page: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Ddsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled, offering 3780 GB of local disk capacity, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance across multiple domains. The server achieves top-tier status in static web serving throughput. It registers strong performance in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, Passmark CPU metrics, and Redis SET operations. However, a notable performance tradeoff is observed in the Passmark memory score, which falls into the bottom 25%, contrasting with its strong cached memory bandwidth performance.

Given its hardware profile and benchmark results, this server is suited for database operations, web serving, and software compilation. The presence of Advanced Matrix Extensions and Advanced Vector Extensions supports workloads like LLM inference, where it demonstrates strong prompt processing speeds. The high local storage capacity combined with a balanced CPU-to-RAM ratio provides solid resource density for general-purpose workloads requiring fast local disk access.
description: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v6 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine from the Ddsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs, 32 physical cores, and 256.0 GB of memory. The instance includes 3780 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in static web serving throughput and strong results in database operations, software compilation, and LLM inference, aided by Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions. However, Passmark memory benchmarks indicate a performance tradeoff, ranking in the bottom 25% despite strong cached memory bandwidth. This instance is suited for database hosting, web serving, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring substantial local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 3780 GB of local storage, delivering strong database, web serving, and software compilation performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v6 is a 64-vCPU general-purpose VM with 256 GB RAM and 3780 GB local storage, optimized for databases and web serving.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-capacity local storage, and strong web serving throughput.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor.
256.0 GB of system memory paired with 3780 GB of local disk storage.
Top-tier performance in static web serving throughput benchmarks.
Strong performance in database operations and Redis SET execution.
Tradeoff in Passmark memory score, which ranks in the bottom 25%.
Suited for database hosting, web serving, and software compilation.
